display.purpose: Hero name and the boot terminal headline. Mono so every character is on-grid.

headline.purpose: Section titles inside app-section-heading. Sets the page rhythm.

title.purpose: Card titles on featured panels, project cards, blog cards.

body.purpose: Inter sans for prose, descriptions, and contact text. The only place Inter appears.

label.purpose: Every uppercase mono label across the system. Buttons, kickers, navbar links, marquee bands all share this role and the 0.18em tracking.

shadows.name: shadow-glass
shadows.purpose: Resting elevation for any glass panel on the default theme. Atmospheric, never structural. Remaps per theme.

shadows.name: shadow-glow
shadows.purpose: The hover bloom. Tinted with the theme's primary signal so it reads as interactivity, not surface depth. Remaps per theme.

shadows.name: shadow-glow-synthwave
shadows.purpose: Synthwave's stronger neon variant. Synthwave doubles down on bloom because its 'material' is light, not weight.

shadows.name: shadow-print-paper
shadows.purpose: The single hairline shadow used on the print theme. Reads as a sheet of paper resting on a desk.

motion.name: ease-bridge-out
motion.purpose: Default exponential ease-out for kinetic-heading and most state transitions. Settles fast then crawls; never bounces.

motion.name: duration-hover
motion.purpose: Hover transitions on cards, buttons, links. Fast enough to read as feedback, slow enough to register.

motion.name: duration-card
motion.purpose: Card lift + glow expand on hover.

motion.name: duration-decrypt
motion.purpose: Text-scramble settle time. Capped so screen readers see the final string almost immediately.

motion.name: duration-marquee
motion.purpose: Marquee band scroll loop. Slow enough that it reads as ambient telemetry, not a banner ad.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary action. The single live signal of the page; rare by design.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; background: var(--accent-primary, #00f0ff); color: var(--bg-primary, #0a0a0f); font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 8px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: box-shadow 200ms cubic-bezier(0.2,0.8,0.2,1), transform 200ms cubic-bezier(0.2,0.8,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { box-shadow: var(--shadow-glow, 0 0 24px rgba(0,240,255,0.25)); transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent-primary, #00f0ff); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Secondary action. Border carries the signal on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; background: var(--bg-surface, #1a1a2e); color: var(--text-primary, #e2e8f0); font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 8px 16px; border: 1px solid var(--border, #334155); border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 200ms, color 200ms; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: var(--accent-primary, #00f0ff); color: var(--accent-primary, #00f0ff); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent-primary, #00f0ff); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Low-emphasis action. Theme toggles, palette triggers, secondary nav actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; background: transparent; color: var(--text-secondary, #94a3b8); font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 8px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms, color 200ms; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: var(--bg-elevated, #2d2d44); color: var(--accent-primary, #00f0ff); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent-primary, #00f0ff); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Tag Chip (Terminal)
components.description: Mono uppercase chip that wraps content in `{ }` braces. The system's verbal tic.
components.css: .ds-chip-terminal { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; background: rgba(45,45,68,0.5); color: var(--accent-primary, #00f0ff); font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 2px 8px; border: 1px solid var(--border, #334155); border-radius: 6px; } .ds-chip-terminal__brace { color: var(--text-muted, #64748b); }

components.name: Glass Panel
components.description: The system's primary container. Translucent skin, hairline edge, hover-glow on lift.
components.html:
FILE 01live

force pop

A click-the-floating-bubbles micro-game built on the same D3 force simulation that powers the hero graph.
components.css: .ds-glass-panel { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 12px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.05); backdrop-filter: blur(12px); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(12px); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.10); border-radius: 16px; padding: 24px; box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.3); transition: border-color 200ms, transform 300ms cubic-bezier(0.2,0.8,0.2,1), box-shadow 300ms cubic-bezier(0.2,0.8,0.2,1); } .ds-glass-panel:hover { border-color: rgba(0,240,255,0.45); transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 10px 40px rgba(0,240,255,0.12); } .ds-glass-panel__head { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.7rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.2em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--text-muted, #64748b); } .ds-glass-panel__index { color: var(--accent-primary, #00f0ff); } .ds-glass-panel__status { padding: 1px 9px; border-radius: 9999px; background: rgba(0,240,255,0.12); border: 1px solid rgba(0,240,255,0.30); color: var(--accent-primary, #00f0ff); } .ds-glass-panel__title { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-weight: 700; font-size: 1.6rem; letter-spacing: -0.02em; color: var(--text-primary, #e2e8f0); margin: 0; } .ds-glass-panel__body { font-family: 'Inter Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.9rem; line-height: 1.6; color: var(--text-secondary, #94a3b8); margin: 0; }

components.name: Section Heading
components.description: Mono kicker leading with `~$`, mono display title under it.
components.html:
~$ playground

D3 demos

Seven small scenes that exercise the same simulation engine the hero is built on.
components.css: .ds-section-heading { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; align-items: flex-start; text-align: left; } .ds-section-heading__kicker { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.2em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--accent-primary, #00f0ff); } .ds-section-heading__prompt { color: var(--text-muted, #64748b); } .ds-section-heading__title { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-weight: 700; font-size: 1.875rem; letter-spacing: -0.02em; color: var(--text-primary, #e2e8f0); margin: 0; } .ds-section-heading__subtitle { font-family: 'Inter Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.6; color: var(--text-secondary, #94a3b8); max-width: 28rem; margin: 0; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Single-line input. Border stays neutral; focus ring carries the signal.
components.css: .ds-input-wrap { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; } .ds-input-label { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--text-secondary, #94a3b8); } .ds-input { background: var(--bg-surface, #1a1a2e); color: var(--text-primary, #e2e8f0); font-family: 'Inter Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.5; padding: 8px 12px; border: 1px solid var(--border, #334155); border-radius: 6px; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--text-muted, #64748b); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent-primary, #00f0ff); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Navbar
components.description: Sticky glass nav, mono `~$ rahul` logo, mono uppercase links.
components.html:
~$ rahul
  • about
  • projects
  • playground
  • feed
  • contact
components.css: .ds-navbar { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; height: 56px; padding: 0 16px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.05); backdrop-filter: blur(12px); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(12px); border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.10); } .ds-navbar__logo { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.2em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--accent-primary, #00f0ff); text-decoration: none; } .ds-navbar__prompt { color: var(--text-muted, #64748b); } .ds-navbar__links { list-style: none; display: flex; gap: 24px; margin: 0; padding: 0; } .ds-navbar__links a { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--text-secondary, #94a3b8); text-decoration: none; transition: color 200ms; } .ds-navbar__links a:hover, .ds-navbar__links a.is-active { color: var(--accent-primary, #00f0ff); }

components.name: Marquee Band
components.description: Horizontal mono ASCII strip that scrolls between scenes. Decorative, aria-hidden.
components.html: // SCENE 02 / projects// RUNTIME 1m24s// BUILD #043// SCENE 02 / projects// RUNTIME 1m24s// BUILD #043
components.css: .ds-marquee { position: relative; width: 100%; overflow: hidden; border-block: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.10); background: rgba(255,255,255,0.05); font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; line-height: 1; color: var(--text-muted, #64748b); letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase; user-select: none; pointer-events: none; } .ds-marquee__track { display: inline-flex; white-space: nowrap; padding-block: 9px; animation: ds-marquee-scroll 30s linear infinite; will-change: transform; } .ds-marquee__item { padding-inline: 10px; } @keyframes ds-marquee-scroll { from { transform: translateX(0); } to { transform: translateX(-50%); } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-marquee__track { animation: none; transform: translateX(0); } }

components.name: Boot Terminal Line
components.description: Single line of the auto-typing boot sequence. Past lines fade to console-dim, the active line carries the cursor.
components.html:
~$ boot --user=rahul
[ OK ] mounting profile…
[ OK ] loading projects (8/8)
~$ rahul --start▌
press any key to skip
components.css: .ds-boot { width: 100%; max-width: 38rem; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1.55; color: var(--text-primary, #e2e8f0); padding: 24px; background: var(--bg-primary, #0a0a0f); } .ds-boot__line { white-space: pre-wrap; } .ds-boot__line--past { color: var(--text-muted, #64748b); } .ds-boot__cursor { display: inline-block; margin-left: 1px; color: var(--accent-primary, #00f0ff); animation: ds-boot-blink 1s steps(2, start) infinite; } .ds-boot__hint { color: var(--text-muted, #64748b); font-size: 10px; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase; opacity: 0.7; margin-top: 12px; } @keyframes ds-boot-blink { to { opacity: 0; } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-boot__cursor { animation: none; } }

narrative.overview:
The interface is the instrument panel; the work is what you are flying. Every visible surface reads as telemetry: HUD chrome around the force graph, corner brackets on featured panels, a marquee runtime ticker between scenes, decrypt animations on section headings, a boot terminal that auto-types the first contact. The visitor is not browsing a portfolio; they are stepping onto a small bridge and watching its instruments come alive.

The system is built on glass over void. The default theme is dark, translucent, and backlit by a single cyan signal. Three alternate theme materials snap onto the same chrome: a high-contrast green CRT (terminal), a quiet ink-on-paper boss-mode (print), and a saturated neon nightscape (synthwave). The chrome is constant across all four; only the material changes.

Density is medium: type is generous, spacing has rhythm, scenes breathe. The site is theatrical, but never cluttered. Cinematic touches (grain, scan-lines, decrypt, kinetic-rise, marquee) are atmospheric layers, never decoration. They are dialed per-theme through --cinematic-* custom properties, and print deliberately turns all of them off so the site can be shared as a clean reference.
keyCharacteristics: Four theme materials, one constant chrome.
Cinematic atmosphere as a CSS-variable dial, not as decoration.
`~$` prompt mark and `{ }` braces as the system's verbal tics.
Hover lifts and glow trails over hard drop-shadows.
Reduced-motion is fully honored; the boss-mode `print` theme strips the show.

rules.name: The Theme-Material Rule
rules.body: Each of the four themes uses a different physics to communicate depth: glass uses backdrop-filter blur and translucent fills, terminal uses contrast borders only (radius 0, no glass), print uses ink weight and one subtle drop-shadow, synthwave uses neon glow as the elevation mechanism. Never port a shadow recipe across themes. Tune depth in the material the theme is made of.

rules.name: The One-Signal Rule
rules.body: The cyan accent is the live signal of the bridge. It appears on roughly 5–10% of any visible surface. Multiplying it weakens the signal; restraint is the point. If a screen needs more emphasis, change typeset or composition, not color count.

rules.name: The `~$` Prompt Rule
rules.body: Every section kicker, the navbar logo, the footer prompt, and most interactive labels lead with the literal token `~$ ` (mono, text-text-muted on the `~$`, text-accent-primary on the term that follows). This is the system's verbal tic; it is how the chrome speaks. New components that need a kicker must use it. Do not invent a different prefix.

rules.name: The Mono-First Rule
rules.body: Headings use the display (JetBrains Mono) face in three of the four themes; only print swaps to serif. Sans is for prose, never for headlines, never for buttons, never for labels.

rules.name: The 0.18em Tracking Rule
rules.body: Every uppercase mono label uses letter-spacing: 0.18em. Buttons, kickers, navbar links, marquee bands all share the same loose tracking. It is the system's audio signature.

rules.name: The Hover-Glows-Not-Lifts Rule
rules.body: On hover, surfaces translate -2px on Y and their box-shadow expands to shadow-glow (a tinted halo). They do not gain a heavier drop-shadow; they bloom. The lift is small enough to read as feedback, not as theatrics.

rules.name: The Print-Mode Rule
rules.body: The print theme deliberately disables grain, scan-lines, glow, and most cinematics. It is the boss-mode reference theme. New cinematic features must check the print matrix before shipping; if a feature looks broken in print, the right answer is usually to disable it for that theme.

rules.name: The Brace Rule
rules.body: The terminal variant of tag-chip wraps its content between `{` and `}` characters in console-dim. This is one of the system's two verbal tics (the other is `~$`). New chip variants in cinematic contexts must adopt it.

rules.name: The Three-Layer Component Rule
rules.body: Every page composition uses three layers: (1) instrument-grade primitives (mono buttons, glass panels, sans body); (2) cinematic chrome around them (HUD corner brackets, decrypt headers, marquee bands, scene frames); (3) ceremonial transitions between states (kinetic-rise, decrypt-resolve, hover-glow, scroll-snap on home/about). All three or nothing.

dos: Do use signal-cyan (#00f0ff) for the single primary action and the focus ring on every public surface.
Do lead every kicker, navbar logo, footer prompt, and label-style mono string with `~$`.
Do wrap terminal-variant tag-chip content between `{` and `}` braces rendered in console-dim.
Do use letter-spacing: 0.18em on every uppercase mono label.
Do use mono (JetBrains Mono Variable) for headings on glass, terminal, and synthwave. print is the only theme allowed to swap to serif.
Do glow on hover (shadow-glow + translateY(-2px)), never deepen the drop-shadow.
Do dial cinematic intensity per theme via the --cinematic-* custom properties.
Do check the print matrix before shipping any new cinematic feature.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion: reduce on every animation.
Do keep brand cinematics off /admin/*. Productivity over performance on the operator's side.
Do mark every decorative overlay with aria-hidden="true" and pointer-events: none.
Do cap body line length at 65–75ch on long-form pages.
donts: Don't use #000 or #fff directly. Reach for void-black or console-bright; every neutral is tinted.
Don't ship a generic developer-portfolio template (avatar + bio + project grid + tech-stack icons).
Don't ship a SaaS landing-page cliché (gradient hero, big metric, three identical feature cards, social-proof logo strip).
Don't ship an off-the-shelf cyberpunk-neon look (solid black + magenta glow + Matrix-rain decoration).
Don't ship a corporate-resume page (beige, polite, 'I am passionate about clean code').
Don't use a side-stripe border (border-left or border-right > 1px as a colored accent).
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text over a gradient background).
Don't use glassmorphism decoratively. The glass class is structural; do not blur things just because.
Don't stack a third elevated surface beyond page → glass panel.
Don't invent a new theme to solve a layout problem. Four themes is the ceiling.
Don't carry the `~$` prompt or the `{ }` braces into /admin/*.
Don't animate CSS layout properties. Use transform and opacity.
Don't add a colored shadow on print. The print theme's --shadow-glow is none for a reason.
Don't preload audio assets. Ambient drone and UI sfx are off by default and lazy-loaded only on the first toggle.
Don't rely on hue alone in data viz.
Don't use bouncy / elastic easing. Bounce reads as consumer-app, not instrument.
